Why are you interested in this role?: I genuinely like being the person who keeps recruiting operations running smoothly while making sure every candidate has a great experience. At InGenius Prep I coordinate scheduling and logistics for 20+ programs across cross-functional teams, and the part I enjoy most is keeping the data and documentation clean enough that nothing slips. A Recruitment Coordinator role at a fast, ambitious company like Lovable is that instinct made into a job—and I love that it sits at the intersection of operational rigor and genuine care for people.
Tell us about your recruiting operations: I have extensive Greenhouse administration experience—posting roles, scheduling interviews, emailing candidates, and keeping the pipeline and data clean. Good ATS hygiene is exactly the kind of detail work I'm relentless about; my supervisor has called me the most organized person he's ever worked with. I'll be candid that I haven't used Ashby specifically yet, but I pick up new platforms quickly and enjoy it, so I'm confident it would click fast.
How do you think about using AI in recru: I'm genuinely excited about this. I use AI tools daily, and I've actually been building a RAG-based system on the side that evaluates job fit from my own resumes and experience—so I think about AI concretely, not abstractly. In recruiting, I see the biggest unlock as removing tedious, repetitive work (scheduling logic, drafting templated comms, surfacing data insights) so coordinators can spend more time on the human, high-judgment parts of candidate experience. That's the kind of smarter ops I'd love to help build at Lovable.
Why Lovable?: I'm drawn to Lovable because you're building something genuinely new—letting anyone turn an idea into real software—and because a small, high-ownership team is exactly where a coordinator's work is visible and compounding. I value extreme ownership and moving fast, and I'd be excited to be the person who keeps your recruiting engine running flawlessly while it scales. My training in Applied Organizational Psychology means I treat process as a system to study and improve, which fits how Lovable seems to work.
